items.title: FTSE 100 Live: UK Economy Grew Unexpectedly in May
Citigroup’s Indian Clients Shift Focus to Rupee Volatility
items.description: Citigroup Inc. says its Indian corporate clients are expecting the rupee will keep weakening and are looking for products that will enable them to profit from its volatility.

items.title: IEA Sees $6.5 Trillion At Risk if China Imposes Rare-Earth Curbs
items.description: China’s rare earth export curbs could put $6.5 trillion of downstream production outside the country at risk each year if fully implemented, the International Energy Agency warned in a report on rare

items.title: Nickel Hits Three-Week High on Indonesia Supply Risks, Fed View
items.description: Nickel climbed to a three-week high as expectations for Federal Reserve rate hikes faded, while uncertainty over mining policy in top producer Indonesia clouded the supply outlook.

items.title: Fidelity Plans to Rebuild Gold Holdings on Long-Term Bull Case
items.description: Asset manager Fidelity International Ltd. plans at some point to add back gold positions that it cut earlier this year, confident that bullion’s long-term drivers remain intact.

items.title: Syngenta’s $5 Billion HK IPO Is Said to Face Fresh Delays
items.description: Syngenta Group’s planned $5 billion Hong Kong initial public offering is facing delays as the seed and pesticide company waits for better conditions in the agriculture sector, according to people fami

items.title: Asian Gas Prices Rise to Highest Since March on Mideast Tensions
items.description: Asian liquefied natural gas prices rose to the highest since late March as fresh hostilities in the Middle East deepened concerns that shipping through the key Strait of Hormuz will remain disrupted f

items.title: China Seeks to Curb New Bond Sales by LGFVs With Increased Debt
items.description: A major Chinese bond regulator is taking fresh steps to curb debt sales by the nation’s municipal borrowers, according to people familiar with the matter, adding pressure on weaker issuers looking to

items.title: Wheat Holds Near Two-Month High After Black Sea Attacks
items.description: Wheat futures held gains after jumping 5% on Wednesday, as Ukrainian and Russian strikes in the Black Sea threatened a key export route for the warring countries.

items.title: Adani Flagship Draws Mutual Fund Interest as Governance Risks Recede
items.description: Local mutual funds bought Adani Enterprises shares worth 46.60 billion rupees last month.

items.title: Pound Index Hits One-Year High on Mahmood for Chancellor Reports
items.description: A measure of sterling’s strength rose to its highest level in a year on Wednesday following reports that incoming UK Prime Minister Andy Burnham will name Shabana Mahmood as his Chancellor of the Exch

items.title: India Increases Fuel Export Levies as Global Supply Tightens
items.description: India raised taxes on exports of ‌diesel and jet fuel as the collapse of the US-Iran interim peace deal and renewed hostilities choked oil flows and tightened the outlook for fuel supply.

items.title: Japan PE Fund Seeks $1.9 Billion Economic-Security Investments
items.description: Advantage Partners LLC plans to invest in big domestic companies posing economic security risks for Japan that might benefit from government backing and boost profits in the long term.

items.title: Korea Plans Measures on Leveraged ETFs Driving Wild Volatility
items.description: South Korea will soon announce measures regarding the leveraged exchange-traded funds tied to shares of Samsung Electronics Co. and SK Hynix Inc. that have fueled a surge in market volatility.

items.title: Global Investors Hunt For Creative Backdoors Into China’s Mega CXMT IPO
items.description: Global investors largely shut out of China’s biggest IPO in nearly four years are looking at creative proxy trades as a way to profit from the country’s fast-rising memory-chip leader.

items.title: Gold Declines as Fresh US Strikes on Iran Fuel Inflation Fears
items.description: Gold declined, erasing a moderate gain from the previous session, as escalating tensions in the Middle East fueled concerns that energy prices and inflation will stay high.

items.title: BHP Posts Solid Full-Year Output, Sees Copper Decline Ahead
items.description: BHP Group Ltd. posted solid full-year production despite a weaker final quarter, though it warned copper output will fall in the year ahead as grades decline at its flagship Chilean mines.

items.title: Stocks Drop as Chip Selloff Hits Kospi, Oil Slips: Markets Wrap
items.description: Semiconductor stocks fell across Asia, dragging regional equities lower, as investors became more skeptical that the artificial intelligence-driven rally can withstand lofty valuations. Oil declined.

items.title: Latest Oil Market News and Analysis for July 16
items.description: Oil steadied after a three-day rally as the US carried out another round of airstrikes on Iran following a series of shipping attacks by the Islamic Republic this week.

items.title: Kalshi Seeks to Let Traders Bet on Flight Cancellation Rates
items.description: Your flight? Canceled. The silver lining? You might be able to make some money off the misery.
